fact: March 7 is exactly 66 days into the year - that's the same number as Route 66!
In the Northern Hemisphere, March 7 falls smack in the middle of when maple trees start making their sweet, sticky sap that becomes syrup!
March 7 is prime time for seeing the Aurora Borealis because geomagnetic activity peaks during equinox season!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 7 is when many spiders start spinning their biggest webs before winter hits!
March 7 is when hibernating animals like groundhogs and bears start getting seriously restless in their dens!
In cold places, March 7 is peak 'sugar snow' season - when snow crystals form perfect hexagonal shapes because of the specific temperature!
The zodiac switches from Pisces to Aries right around March 7, making it one of the most 'transitional' days of the year!
In many northern places, March 7 typically falls in 'mud season' - that gross but amazing time when frozen ground turns into the squishiest, stickiest mess!
